factors.name: Fed Policy (Warsh debut)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh held rates steady but signaled potential hike later in 2026; dot plot shifted to ~50% December hike probability (up from 30%). Removal of forward guidance creates volatility and supports higher USD, which pressures gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed pivot and rate-hike expectations lift real yields, the single most correlated negative driver for gold. No specific TIPS level in news, but implied upward pressure from Warsh's tone and September/December hike odds.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakened post US-Iran MoU signing as risk sentiment improved; EUR/USD rallied to 1.1515, GBP/USD recovered from 2-month lows, AUD/USD above 0.7025. Weaker USD structure is BULLISH for gold, contradicting Fed hawkishness.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran MoU)
factors.summary: US and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding to end war, including reopening of Strait of Hormuz and $300bn redevelopment package. This dramatically reduces Middle East geopolitical premium, lowering oil and reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI holds above $75.10 but sentiment weak; IEA forecasts supply glut next year post-Iran deal; Goldman warns base case $80 Brent year-end. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = less Fed hike urgency but also reduces inflation-hedge demand for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Market
factors.summary: Asia stocks surge (Nikkei, KOSPI at record highs); NZD, AUD, GBP all strengthen on improved risk sentiment post-Iran deal. Risk-on favors equities over safe-haven gold and weakens USD relative to commodity-linked currencies.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in this window. Warsh's hawkish stance and uncertainty (no forward guidance) keeps 2026-28 inflation expectations elevated, but Iran peace and likely lower oil reduce near-term inflation impulse. Mixed signals.

factors.name: BoJ & Yen Carry
factors.summary: Japan's Kihara flags weak yen burden but offers no fresh intervention signal; USD/JPY consolidates above 160.00, highest since July 2024. BoJ remains dovish but intervention risk is present, creating volatility; unclear near-term direction.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh data on GLD, IAU holdings or PBoC/RBI buying in this window. Structural bid from central banks typically supportive, but not enough to counter hawkish Fed + risk-on dynamics.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold slumped to near $4,250 post-FOMC (per FXStreet 23:18 report), now recovering to $4,339.70 (+0.27% in 4h, -0.15% in 24h). Move is shallow and choppy; initial selling from Warsh hawk signal has partially reversed on USD weakness and Iran peace.

screening.narrative: Gold faced a sharp selloff immediately post-FOMC when Warsh signaled potential rate hikes in 2H 2026 and removed forward guidance, pushing real yields higher and creating uncertainty. However, the US-Iran peace deal and subsequent risk-on sentiment have weakened the USD and lifted equity markets, partially offsetting the bearish Fed impulse. The micro-structure shows gold caught between hawkish Fed policy (bearish for gold, bullish for USD) and geopolitical de-escalation + improving risk sentiment (bullish for risk assets, bearish for safe-haven USD, bullish for gold via weaker dollar). This creates a shallow, choppy recovery from session lows—classic Phase 2 indecision.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI data (if scheduled in next 72h—check calendar for release date)
US Retail Sales / ISM Manufacturing / Services PMI
PBOC policy signals or yuan fixing pattern (currently allowing modest depreciation, 6.8130 vs 6.7752 estimate suggests soft weaken)
Japan intervention on USD/JPY if yen breaks lower again (risk factor, not baseline)
Fed speakers (Warsh, other FOMC members) elaborating on new framework and rate-path assumptions
Oil price action and OPEC+ response to Iran reopening/supply fears
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation of NEUTRAL bias: (1) If Fed speakers over next 48h walk back rate-hike signals or signal 'no hikes 2026', real yields would fall sharply and gold would rally decisively. (2) If Iran deal unravels or Netanyahu/Israeli escalation occurs, geopolitical premium snaps back, oil spikes, and safe-haven gold rallies. (3) If US economic data (NFP, ISM) disappoints materially, risk sentiment shifts to flight-to-safety, weakening dollar and supporting gold above $4,350. Conversely, if Fed hawkishness is reaffirmed and risk-on reverses, gold breaks $4,250 and tests $4,200.
screening.entry_guidance: Move extended 3+ hours post-FOMC with only shallow rebound; avoid chasing at current levels. Wait for retest of $4,280–$4,300 support or a break above $4,350 on fresh catalyst (e.g. soft inflation data or dovish Fed speaker) before entering with conviction. If risk sentiment fades and USD rebounds, support $4,250 is your next buy zone.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed repricing by new Chair Kevin Warsh triggered a shift to 50%+ probability of rate hike by October (up from 30%), pushing 2-year Treasury yields sharply higher. Higher nominal yields with sticky inflation expectations = rising real yields, which is the single largest headwind for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: FOMC decision showed a more aggressive dot plot under Warsh leadership; markets fully priced a 2026 rate hike. This hawkish surprise is directly bearish gold, evidenced by simultaneous selloff in both gold and Bitcoin as yields surged post-FOMC.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed repricing has supported broad USD strength. EUR/USD weakened to ~1.1475–1.1525 lows, USD/JPY remained near 160.80, and USD/CAD traded above 1.4100. Stronger dollar directly competes with gold and is a headwind.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium (Oil/Safe-Haven)
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire/MoU signed; oil fell to 3-month lows sub-$74 (WTI) as supply glut fears rose and Strait of Hormuz reopened. Lower oil reduces inflation expectations and geopolitical fear premium, which normally supports gold but is being overwhelmed by the hawkish Fed.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Action
factors.summary: Risk assets initially rallied on Iran deal (oil down, stocks up in Asia/Europe early), but equity rally lost steam as hawkish Fed fears dominated. European shares edged lower on Fed rate-hike bets; this risk-off backdrop should support gold but is being suppressed by real yield headwinds.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE data in this window. UK employment data beat expectations (4.9% vs 5.0% unemployment, wage growth +4.4% vs +4.0% y/y), which is slightly inflationary and supports the Fed's hawkish tilt. SNB inflation forecast nudged higher (2026: 0.6% vs 0.5% prior).

factors.name: Other Central Banks (SNB, BoE, BoJ)
factors.summary: SNB kept policy rate at 0% as expected, maintaining heightened forex intervention readiness. BoE expected to hold at 3.75% later today; hawkish Fed repricing is overshadowing these central banks. BoJ weak-yen commentary continues without fresh intervention signals.

factors.name: Gold Technicals & Price Action
factors.summary: Gold retreated from $4,300+ intraday high; currently down 0.59% on 4h, 0.77% on 24h. Despite retaining 'intraday bullish bias' per FXStreet at $4,300, the broader move is down into hawkish Fed repricing. Price action suggests early-phase downward retest underway.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: Stronger USD and rising real yields are attracting flows away from gold into US Treasuries. Swiss franc weakened post-SNB (0% hold), so CHF is not competing. Bitcoin also sold off with gold post-FOMC.

screening.narrative: Gold is under acute pressure from an unexpected hawkish Fed pivot under new Chair Kevin Warsh, with markets repricing a 50%+ probability of a 2026 rate hike by October. Real yields are rising sharply (nominal yields up, inflation expectations sticky), the dollar is rallying, and safe-haven flows are rotating into US fixed income rather than bullion. The US-Iran ceasefire removes geopolitical risk premium (oil down to 3-month lows), which normally supports gold, but this tailwind is completely overwhelmed by the real-yield and Fed hawkishness headwinds. Gold has already declined ~0.77% in 24h, putting it into early Phase 2 of a down-move; the technical backdrop suggests further testing of $4,250–$4,200 support is likely near-term.
catalysts_ahead: BoE rate decision (June 18, later today) — expected hold; mixed signals from UK jobs beat may reinforce pause bias
Any Fed speakers (Warsh, Waller, etc.) — further hawkish commentary would extend gold downside
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — if data softens, could spark a relief rally in gold; if hot, confirms hawkish trajectory
Next FOMC meeting (July 2026) — full-rate decision cycle now in focus; any Powell commentary critical
Oil price stabilization — if WTI stabilizes above $75, geopolitical premium may re-emerge and provide modest gold support
screening.risk_warning: If real yields reverse (e.g., market reprices Fed pivot back to cuts), or if fresh geopolitical shock (Israel, Russia, or other conflict escalation) reignites safe-haven demand, gold could quickly reverse and test $4,350+ resistance. Additionally, any dovish pivot from Warsh or other Fed officials would be sharply bullish gold. Conversely, if CPI prints hot in the coming weeks, downside to $4,100–$4,000 becomes realistic.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (Phase 2); wait for a retest of $4,280–$4,300 resistance or a pullback from $4,250 support before establishing fresh shorts. Longs should avoid chasing here; look for evidence of Fed policy reversal or recession fears before considering bullish exposure.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: Fed delivered hawkish hold with Warsh's dot plot showing more aggressive stance. Markets now fully pricing Fed hike by October (probability jumped from 30% to 50%+ per Deutsche Bank), pushing real yields higher.

factors.name: Real Yields

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium
factors.summary: US-Iran memorandum of understanding signed, ending war. Oil falling hard (WTI down 10% weekly to $73.36, three-month lows), Strait of Hormuz reopening. Major de-escalation removes safe-haven bid that supported gold at elevated levels.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: Brent/WTI collapsing on Iran deal and supply glut forecasts (IEA). US gasoline below $4/gallon for first time since March. Lower oil reduces inflation expectations, removes tail-risk premium from gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Mixed signals: equities sold off post-FOMC, but China stocks near bear market. VIX elevated. Gold caught between safe-haven demand (equity weakness) and dollar/yield strength (Fed hawkish). Net neutral as flows conflicted.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (ex-Fed)
factors.summary: SNB held rates at 0% as expected, BoE expected to hold at 3.75% today. No major dovish surprises to offset Fed hawkishness. BoJ still passive despite yen near intervention levels.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Collapsing oil prices and Europe gas falling sixth consecutive session will pull near-term inflation expectations lower, reducing inflation-hedge appeal of gold even as Fed turns hawkish.

factors.name: Recent Price Action
factors.summary: Gold down -0.77% in 24h, already declined from recent highs. Move is in progress but not yet extended. Trading near $4,300 after touching highs above $4,350 earlier this week.

factors.name: Competing Safe Havens
factors.summary: USD strengthening as primary safe-haven on Fed hawkishness. Bitcoin also declined alongside gold (per Deutsche Bank), suggesting broad de-risking from alternative stores of value into cash/short-duration bonds.

confirmation.narrative: Gold faces a bearish confluence as the hawkish Fed pivot under new Chair Warsh drives real yields and the dollar higher, while the US-Iran peace deal simultaneously removes the geopolitical risk premium that supported prices at elevated levels. The combination of higher opportunity cost (rising real yields) and lower tail-risk (Middle East de-escalation, collapsing oil) creates a two-sided squeeze. Price has already begun declining (-0.77% in 24h) but the move is not yet extended, suggesting further downside potential toward $4,250 support as markets continue repricing Fed hike expectations and oil's decline filters through to lower inflation expectations.
catalysts_ahead: BoE rate decision today (expected hold at 3.75%, watch for any hawkish tilt on wage data)
UK employment data just released showed stronger jobs/wages, supporting BoE patience
US weekly jobless claims Thursday
Further developments on US-Iran MoU implementation and Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline
Any Fed speaker follow-up to clarify Warsh's framework overhaul
China stimulus measures monitoring (third trade-in tranche announced, but retail sales weak)
confirmation.risk_warning: Invalidation if: (1) Fed speakers walk back hawkish dot plot or Warsh clarifies dovish framework shift, (2) US-Iran deal collapses and geopolitical tensions re-escalate rapidly, (3) major equity selloff accelerates triggering flight-to-quality into gold despite strong dollar, (4) US data deteriorates sharply forcing Fed to abandon hike talk. Near-term risk: gold at $4,302 is still historically elevated; if $4,250 support breaks decisively, could see accelerated liquidation toward $4,150-4,200.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway but not extended; consider short entries on rallies toward $4,320-4,330 resistance with stops above $4,350, targeting $4,250 initially.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed expectations are driving real yields higher as markets price ~44bp of Fed tightening by Q2 2026. Higher real yields are the single most bearish driver for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)

factors.name: US-Iran Deal (Geopolitical Relief)
factors.summary: Trump administration signed interim MoU with Iran, extending ceasefire 60 days and reopening Strait of Hormuz. Removes safe-haven demand and long-dated geopolitical premium that had supported gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: Oil is falling sharply on Iran deal reducing supply risk; gasoline just fell below $4/gallon. Lower energy prices reduce inflation risk, which reduces Fed tightening urgency—but immediate relief removes inflation hedge bid for gold.

factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Bond traders dumped short-term Treasuries and piled into bets for rate hikes as soon as next month after Warsh's hawkish messaging. This inversion of expectations (from hold to hikes) is sharply bearish for non-yielding gold.

factors.name: Treasury Yields (Nominal)
factors.summary: Warsh's comments triggered a Treasury selloff (front-end yields spiked), though some rebound occurred later. Higher nominal yields = higher opportunity cost of holding gold.

factors.name: Other CB Policy (BoE, SNB, Norges)

factors.name: Recent Price Action (24h decline)

screening.narrative: Gold is in a clear downtrend following a hawkish Fed surprise (dot plot projecting rate hikes in 2026) and Kevin Warsh's debut as Fed Chair, which signalled zero tolerance for inflation and shifted market pricing toward multiple tightening cycles. Simultaneously, the US-Iran deal removes geopolitical safe-haven premium and oil prices are collapsing, reducing inflation hedging demand. Real yields are grinding higher as nominal yields spike and inflation expectations ease. The USD is at its strongest since May 2025, creating a double headwind. Gold is in PHASE_2: the selloff has begun and is gathering momentum, though it may be approaching a near-term exhaustion point.

screening.narrative: Gold sold off 2.53% in 24h on the back of a sharp hawkish repricing of Fed rate expectations following Kevin Warsh's hawkish tilt Wednesday. The US-Iran peace deal and Hormuz reopening simultaneously triggered de-risking flows (equities +1%, safe-haven bid unwound) and an oil price collapse, both bearish for gold's inflation hedge narrative. The USD rallied to YTD highs, completing a perfect bearish setup: higher real yields (via higher nominal yields), stronger dollar, lower oil, and fading geopolitical risk. Gold's move is already in progress (down >2% in 24h), signaling PHASE 2 selling with momentum to the downside.
catalysts_ahead: US inflation data (CPI/Core CPI release if scheduled within 24-72h; monitor for persistence of disinflationary signals post-oil collapse)
Fed speakers: monitor for additional hawkish rhetoric from Waller, Barkin, other FOMC members
US-Iran MOU implementation details and escalation risk (could flip sentiment back to risk-off if any violations emerge)
Treasury auction calendar and longer-duration yields
Oil market reaction to Hormuz reopening and OPEC response (watch WTI/Brent for stabilization or further collapse)
screening.risk_warning: If real yields roll over sharply (e.g., 10y TIPS yield drops >15 bps from current levels), or if geopolitical tensions reignite (Iran compliance failure, Israel response, Ukraine escalation), safe-haven flows could reverse and reignite the bid. Additionally, if upcoming CPI/inflation data prints hot (contrary to oil-led disinflationary narrative), the Fed's hawkish tilt could be questioned and rates could fall, bullish gold. Watch for mean reversion in USD/JPY if carry trades unwind.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (already down 2.53% in 24h); avoid aggressive shorts now. Wait for a pullback/retest of $4,280–$4,300 resistance before shorting again, or enter on a break below $4,200 support with tight stops above $4,250. Consider lightening short exposure if real yields gap lower or if Iran deal breaks down within 24–48h.
